name: Hercules Globular Cluster
shortDescription: A massive ball-shaped cluster containing hundreds of thousands of ancient stars.
description: Messier 3, often called M3, is one of the brightest and most impressive globular clusters visible from Earth. It contains hundreds of thousands of stars packed tightly together in a spherical shape. Many of the stars in M3 are extremely old, making this object a glimpse into the early history of our galaxy.
whatYouAreSeeing: You are looking at a dense swarm of ancient stars held together by gravity. Unlike open clusters, globular clusters are much older and contain far more stars packed into a relatively small area.
whyItIsInteresting: M3 is interesting because it is one of the best examples of a globular cluster visible in the northern sky. It helps show how different star clusters can look and gives a sense of the enormous scale of our galaxy.
captureNotes: Captured as part of a beginner astrophotography journey using the Seestar S30. Globular clusters are excellent beginner deep-sky targets because they are bright and easy to locate.
